messages.content:
What is a side-benefit of involving multiple team members in the ideation process?
Select an answer:
It creates a sense of shared ownership of the solution.
Each team member learns new design skills.
It ensures that everyone is kept busy during this phase of the process.
It gives insight into how each team member thinks.

The side-benefit of involving multiple team members in the ideation process is:

It creates a sense of shared ownership of the solution.

Your company's current product line is mature, and you are looking to expand into new areas. Which user-centered approach is most useful?
Select an answer:
content testing
usability testing
information architecture research
field research and ethnography
Field research and ethnography

How can you best demonstrate the success of your design thinking process to your executives?
Select an answer:
Run a team member satisfaction survey.
Ask some customers what they think of the new product when it's released.
Show the financial impact of each of your metrics.
The results of the project should speak for themselves.
Show the financial impact of each of your metrics.

What is a central benefit of design thinking?
Select an answer:
Business and technology team members won't have to work together.
You can bypass the technology-centric individuals on the team and focus just on the more creative ones.
Business representatives can dictate the approach that will be taken.
It helps technology-centric individuals understand customer issues.
It helps technology-centric individuals understand customer issues.

What might best persuade people from different disciplines that it's worth their while to attend the design thinking exercise?
Select an answer:
Persuade their managers to force them to attend.
Tell them there will be donuts.
Let them know that being involved now lets everyone have their say.
Explain that the choice is to spend some concentrated planning time up front, or multiple meetings later on.
Let them know that being involved now lets everyone have their say.

What is the planning output from the design thinking process?
Select an answer:
a step-by-step plan for building out each section of the product in turn
a set of interface sketches that the development team can use to create a story backlog
an incremental plan that delivers core functionality early, adding functionality in frequent iterative releases
a Gantt chart with time and resource estimates for each feature you plan to implement
an incremental plan that delivers core functionality early, adding functionality in frequent iterative releases

What's the best way to sell design thinking to influential individuals in your organization?
Select an answer:
Meet one-on-one and focus on the one benefit of the process that most helps each individual.
Send out a meeting request and hope that everyone shows up.
Bring them all into a room together for a three-hour presentation on the benefits of design thinking.
Call them on the telephone and tell them you need their involvement.
Meet one-on-one and focus on the one benefit of the process that most helps each individual.

How does design thinking help dispersed or outsourced teams?
Select an answer:
Team members can each submit their own ideas of how the design should work.
The artifacts paint a picture of the customer interaction so every team member starts from the same understanding.
Design thinking requires frequent check-ins so everyone communicates better.
Because design thinking happens so fast, nobody has a chance to question the decisions.
The artifacts paint a picture of the customer interaction so every team member starts from the same understanding.

What does the user testing and feedback part of design thinking give you?
Select an answer:
an opportunity to test out marketing messages you might use for your new product
a chance to ask users questions about your current products.
confidence that your concepts are likely to meet users' needs
a chance to justify your changes to users
confidence that your concepts are likely to meet users' needs

What does an iterative development approach help you to do?
Select an answer:
Split the work up into more manageable chunks.
Decide what's best on behalf of customers, so you don't have to build so much.
Identify the most valuable pieces to implement to maximize business and customer benefit.
Spread the work over more releases in case the project gets canceled halfway through.
Identify the most valuable pieces to implement to maximize business and customer benefit.
